rubric: Must note that set() destroys order and may fail on unhashable items; should keep O(n) ordered dedupe or justify trade-offs. Credit for catching both issues, not just one.

prompt:
[AI-assisted · Critique & fix] You may use an AI assistant. An AI produced this SQL for "active users who logged in last 30 days":

```sql
SELECT user_id, MAX(login_at) AS last_login
FROM logins
WHERE login_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
GROUP BY user_id;
```
Your PM also wants users who registered but never logged in. Spot at least two gaps in this query for the stated goal and write corrected SQL or explain the fixes.
rubric: Should identify missing never-logged-in users (needs join to users/registration), timezone/ boundary issues optional; must propose a structurally correct approach, not just restate the AI query.

prompt: [AI-assisted · Critique & fix] You may use an AI assistant. AI drafted an incident postmortem root cause: "Database CPU spiked because queries got slower." Logs show p95 latency rose only after cache eviction during a deploy; error rate stayed flat. Critique the AI root cause and write a sharper causal chain a team could action.
rubric: Rejects circular/vague root cause; ties deploy → cache cold → latency without claiming errors; proposes verification steps. Senior depth: distinguishes symptom vs cause.

prompt: [AI-assisted · Direct & refine] You may use an AI assistant. Task: draft a polite 3-sentence email declining a meeting that conflicts with a hard deadline. Write the prompt you would give the AI, then list three checks you would run on the output before sending.
rubric: Prompt includes tone, length, and decline reason; checks cover tone, factual accuracy (deadline/date), and no over-promising follow-up.

prompt: [AI-assisted · Direct & refine] You may use an AI assistant. You need a concise RFC outline for adding OAuth login to an existing email/password app. Describe your prompt strategy (what context you would paste in) and how you would validate the AI outline against security basics.
rubric: Mentions threat model pieces (token storage, CSRF/state, session migration); prompt strategy is concrete; validation is actionable not generic "read it carefully".

prompt: [AI-assisted · Direct & refine] You may use an AI assistant. Legal asked for a data-retention summary for customer PII across three microservices. Outline how you would decompose the task for AI (chunking, prompts) and what you would never delegate to AI alone.
rubric: Chunking by service/data class; human verification of legal claims; flags that AI may invent retention periods — must keep lawyer/human sign-off explicit.

prompt: [AI-assisted · Judgment] You may use an AI assistant. A teammate wants to paste 200 production customer support tickets into ChatGPT to "find themes" and share the transcript in Slack. Should they? Answer yes/no/mixed, justify, and describe a safer alternative.
rubric: Identifies PII/confidentiality risk; rejects or heavily constrains raw paste; offers redaction, sampling, internal tooling, or aggregated exports.

prompt: [AI-assisted · Judgment] You may use an AI assistant. Your CEO asks you to auto-reject job applicants whose AI-written cover letters score below 70% "human" from a detector. Explain whether you would use AI this way and what you would recommend instead.
rubric: Covers fairness, detector unreliability, false positives, legal/ethical hiring risk; proposes structured work sample or rubric-based review instead of detector gate.

prompt: [AI-assisted · Judgment] You may use an AI assistant. For fixing a one-line typo in a public README, is AI assistance worth it? Briefly say when you would and would not use AI for this class of task.
rubric: Recognizes trivial task may be faster manually; OK to use AI for consistency/checklist; should mention verifying diff and not leaking private repo context if applicable.

prompt: [AI-assisted · Verification] You may use an AI assistant. AI claims: "PostgreSQL MVCC means readers never block writers." Is that accurate? Explain nuance and how you would verify before repeating it in a design doc.
rubric: Notes generally true for plain SELECT vs UPDATE but exceptions (long transactions, vacuum bloat, lock modes); cites docs/experiment — not blind trust.

prompt: [AI-assisted · Verification] You may use an AI assistant. AI summarized a vendor SOC 2 report as "no findings." You only have the PDF executive summary. What would you check before trusting that summary for a procurement decision?
rubric: Ask for full report, scope, period, carve-outs, complementary controls; never rely on AI paraphrase alone for compliance sign-off.

prompt: [AI-assisted · Verification] You may use an AI assistant. AI generated a regex to validate email addresses. Describe two ways the regex could be wrong and how you would test it before shipping.
rubric: Internationalized emails, plus-addressing, over/under matching; proposes test vectors or property tests — concrete not vague.

prompt:
[AI-assisted · Critique & fix] You may use an AI assistant. AI wrote a customer reply about a billing double-charge:

"We've processed a full refund and upgraded you to Enterprise for the inconvenience."

You know policy allows refund only and no plan change without approval. Rewrite the reply and note what you changed and why.
rubric: Removes unauthorized upgrade; empathetic tone; states factual next steps and timeline; flags policy violation in original.

prompt: [AI-assisted · Critique & fix] You may use an AI assistant. AI produced a project plan moving auth rewrite, PCI scope reduction, and a mobile launch into the same two-week sprint. Identify unrealistic assumptions and propose a rebalanced plan (no need for full dates).
rubric: Calls out dependency/risk/compliance sequencing; separates must-ship vs parallelizable; shows judgment on capacity not just "add more time".

prompt: [AI-assisted · Direct & refine] You may use an AI assistant. You have a vague user story: "Make reporting faster." Write an AI prompt that would help you turn it into testable acceptance criteria for an engineering ticket.
rubric: Prompt asks for metrics, baseline, scope, edge cases; output would include measurable criteria — candidate shows they drive specificity.

prompt: [AI-assisted · Verification] You may use an AI assistant. AI says HTTP status 429 always means "try again after 60 seconds." What is incomplete about that advice and how would you confirm retry behavior for an API you integrate?
rubric: Mentions Retry-After header, exponential backoff, idempotency; would read API docs or inspect responses — not universal 60s rule.

prompt: [AI-assisted · Judgment] You may use an AI assistant. A junior engineer used AI to generate 400 lines of Terraform for a new environment overnight. Code review is due in an hour. Describe your review strategy: what do you skim, what do you verify by hand, and what would block merge?
rubric: Prioritizes state backend, IAM blast radius, secrets, network exposure; realistic time-boxing; blocks on security/compliance not style nits.

prompt:
[AI-assisted · Critique & fix] You may use an AI assistant. AI suggested this JavaScript debounce:

`const debounced = (fn) => { let t; return (...a) => { clearTimeout(t); t = setTimeout(fn, 300); }; };`

Explain the bug when `fn` uses arguments or `this`, and provide a corrected version or pattern.
rubric: Must identify lost args/this binding; fix uses apply or passes args; partial credit for explaining stale closure if relevant.

prompt: [AI-assisted · Direct & refine] You may use an AI assistant. Task: summarize a 10-page internal PDF for your manager in 5 bullets. You cannot paste the PDF (confidential). How would you use AI anyway, if at all?
rubric: Recognizes confidentiality constraint; may use AI on self-written notes/outline not raw PDF; or declines paste and summarizes manually — credit for data-handling awareness.

prompt: [AI-assisted · Verification] You may use an AI assistant. AI cites "a 2023 McKinsey study" that AI adoption increases revenue 20% in 6 months. How do you treat that claim in a board slide?
rubric: Requires primary source, date, sample, causation vs correlation; would omit or soften unverified stats; demonstrates epistemic hygiene.

prompt: [AI-assisted · Judgment] You may use an AI assistant. Your team uses AI for first-draft unit tests. When is that helpful vs harmful for a payments module? Give two examples of each.
rubric: Helpful: boilerplate, edge enumeration with review; harmful: crypto/compliance paths trusted blindly; must mention human review and critical-path coverage.

prompt: [AI-assisted · Critique & fix] You may use an AI assistant. AI generated a migration plan dropping a legacy `users.email_normalized` column "because it duplicates email." You know downstream ETL dedupes on that column. Write the review comment you would leave on the PR and the questions you'd ask before approval.
rubric: Blocks blind drop; asks consumers, backfill, rollout order; proposes deprecation phase — shows cross-system thinking.
